title: ExistingAccountPicker Component
content:
The `ExistingAccountPicker` component allows users to select an existing GitHub installation to connect to a pair. It fetches available installations, displays the currently connected account, and provides options to connect a new account or uninstall existing ones.

## Key Exports

- `ExistingAccountPicker`: The main React component.

## Props

- `pairId`: The unique identifier for the integration pair.
- `type`: The type of integration ('target' or 'doc').
- `connected`: A boolean indicating if an account is currently connected.
- `connectedLogin`: The GitHub login of the connected account, if any.
- `disabled`: A boolean to disable the component.
- `disabledReason`: A string explaining why the component is disabled.

## Usage Example

```tsx
<ExistingAccountPicker
  pairId="some-pair-id"
  type="target"
  connected={true}
  connectedLogin="octocat"
/>
```

## Internal Components and Logic

- **`loginLabel(login: string | undefined | null)`**: A helper function to format the GitHub login string.
- **`AccountRow`**: A sub-component used to render individual account rows within the dropdown, displaying the login, slot count, and options to select or uninstall.

## Internal API Endpoints Used

- `/api/github/installations`: Fetches a list of available GitHub installations.
- `/api/github/use-installation`: Connects a selected GitHub installation to the pair.
- `/api/github/connect`: Initiates the GitHub OAuth connection flow for a new account.
- `/api/github/uninstall-app`: Uninstalls the GitHub App for a specific installation.
